channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bhutan's Gross National Happiness Policy and Its Record on Ethnic Expulsions and Human Rights
item.description: Bhutan, a small Himalayan kingdom, has long been praised internationally for pioneering the concept of Gross National Happiness (GNH) as an alternative to GDP-focused economic growth. Introduced by Ki...

item.title: Abhishek Banerjee Raises Security Concerns After Armed Person Detained at Kolkata Airport
item.description: Trinamool Congress (TMC) national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee expressed concern over security arrangements after an alleged armed individual was detained at Kolkata Airport on Friday night, sh...

item.title: Former Federal Prosecutor Questions DOJ Conduct in Broadview Six Case After Release of Transcripts
item.description: Newly released transcripts from the Broadview Six case have prompted criticism from former federal prosecutor Andrew Weissmann, who argued that several actions taken by prosecutors from President Dona...

item.title: Seven People Martyred and Three Injured in Two Explosions Targeting Passenger Van and Rescuers in Bannu
item.description: At least seven people were martyred and three others injured in two separate explosions in the Merka Bera area of Bannu on June 20, 2026, according to local police authorities. District Police Officer...

item.title: Kennedy Center Keeps Tarps in Place After Court-Ordered Removal of Trump's Name
item.description: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts remains at the center of controversy after a federal court ordered the removal of President Donald Trump's name from the building's exterior. The cou...

item.title: Studio Portraits Document the Variety of Women's Hairstyles in the Late 1960s
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 35 vintage studio portraits highlighting women's hairstyles from the late 1960s, a period marked by significant cultural and fashion changes...

item.title: National Assembly committee blocks FBR plan for direct access to taxpayers’ bank account data during Finance Bill review
item.description: The National Assembly Standing Committee on Finance and Revenue has rejected a proposal in the Finance Bill 2026-27 that would have allowed the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) to access taxpayers’ bank...

item.title: New Jersey Council Member John Alite Charged with Loan Sharking, Extortion, and Threats
item.description: John Alite, a Republican member of the Englishtown Borough Council in New Jersey, has been arrested and charged with multiple offenses related to an alleged illegal lending operation. According to cha...

item.title: Support for Trump Among Latino Business Owners Declines Amid Concerns Over Immigration Policies and Tariffs
item.description: A report highlighted a significant decline in support for President Donald Trump among Latino business owners during his second term. According to polling cited in the article, approval among this bus...

item.title: Sekgakgapeng Residents Rely on Donkey Carts and Informal Collection Points Amid Years of Water Shortages
item.description: Residents of Sekgakgapeng, a village near Mokopane in Limpopo, have endured unreliable access to running water for approximately five years, forcing many households to depend on alternative methods to...

item.title: Silk Road Café Dispute Over Dublin Castle Closure Set for Arbitration Hearing
item.description: The legal dispute involving Dublin's Silk Road Café and its landlord, the Chester Beatty Library, is expected to move toward arbitration after the landlord announced plans to seek court approval to re...

item.title: At Least 30 Deaths Reported at Displacement Camp as Ebola Outbreak Intensifies in Eastern Congo
item.description: Health officials and aid organisations are raising concerns that Ebola may be spreading rapidly through a displacement camp in north-eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo after at least 30 people d...

item.title: Republican Strategist Warns Trump-Meloni Rift Could Weaken U.S. Alliances During Global Tensions
item.description: A political dispute between U.S. President Donald Trump and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has drawn concern from Republican strategist Doug Heye, who warned that the fallout could have broader...

item.title: Best British Beaches for a Summer Heatwave: Expert Recommendations Across the UK
item.description: As a heatwave brings warmer weather to the UK, sea temperatures can reach the high teens, making swimming without a wetsuit more appealing. Travel experts from the Daily Mail have compiled a guide to ...

item.title: Actress from 'Weightlifting Fairy Kim Bok Joo' Draws Attention for Noticeably Different Look in New Photos
item.description: The article discusses South Korean actress Jo Hye Jung, known for her role as Jung Nan Hee in the popular K-drama “Weightlifting Fairy Kim Bok Joo,” who recently became a trending topic in Korean medi...

item.title: How Shai Kaplan’s Iyengar Yoga Practice Shaped Her Recovery Journey and Inspired a Hospital Rehabilitation Program
item.description: This feature article recounts the story of Shai Kaplan, an Israeli woman who spent much of her youth battling recurring brain tumors, severe pain, and significant vision loss. After multiple surgeries...

item.title: Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo’s Transgender Sports Ballot Measure Misses the 2026 Ballot
item.description: Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo announced that his proposed ballot initiative concerning transgender athletes in school sports will not appear on the state's 2026 ballot. According to Lombardo, legal del...

item.title: Relativity Space Plans Private Mars Orbiter with NASA Scientific Instruments for 2028 Launch
item.description: Relativity Space, a U.S. aerospace company best known for developing the Terran R launch vehicle scheduled for debut around 2027, has announced an unexpected plan to send a Mars orbiter in 2028. The c...

item.title: USA takes a 2-0 lead over Australia in FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between the United States and Australia at Lumen Field in Seattle saw the hosts establish a comfortable 2-0 advantage. The United States entered the fixture after...

item.title: India–UK CETA to boost wider ties beyond trade, says Foreign Secretary
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri has stated that the India–UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which is set to come into force on July 15, represents much more than a conventional trad...
